entries.title: Commentary on Proverbs on proverbs 10:14
entries.excerpt: The wise hide knowledge. Knowing indeed, that there is a time to be silent and a time to speak (Eccl. III). Hence the prophet says, "While the sinner stood against me, I was mute and humbled and silent from good things" (Psal. XXXVIII). And the Apostle, "I decided to know nothing among you except Jesus Christ, and Him crucified" (1 Cor. II).

entries.title: Commentary on Proverbs on proverbs 10:14
entries.excerpt: But the mouth of the fool is near to confusion. Because either he will speak good things inordinately, or openly speak evil things.
